title: androsace septentrionalis var. intermedia
intro: Androsace septentrionalis var. intermedia, commonly known as the Arctic Rock-jelly, is a variant of the Arctic Androsace, a low-growing, herbaceous perennial plant native to the Arctic regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of consuming Arctic Rock-jelly are attributed to its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions or decoctions, and its extracts have been studied for their potential use in the development of new pharmaceuticals.
